framework.name: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria
framework.description: SOC 2 Type II compliance framework based on AICPA Trust Services Criteria (TSC). Enforces Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy controls in real-time during development.

rules.title: No eval() or dynamic code execution
rules.description: CC6.1 (Logical Access): eval() and new Function() execute arbitrary strings as code, enabling injection attacks. This violates logical access security controls.
rules.fix: Replace eval() with JSON.parse() for data, or use a sandboxed execution context.

rules.title: No hardcoded credentials
rules.description: CC6.1 (Logical Access): Hardcoded secrets in source code bypass access control mechanisms and are exposed to anyone with code access.
rules.fix: Use environment variables, AWS Secrets Manager, or HashiCorp Vault.

rules.title: Async functions must have error handling
rules.description: CC6.1 (Logical Access): Unhandled promise rejections can crash the process, creating availability gaps and potential security bypasses.
rules.fix: Wrap the async function body in try/catch with proper error logging.

rules.title: Network code must have authentication
rules.description: CC6.1 (Logical Access): Files making network requests must include authentication logic to prevent unauthorized access.
rules.fix: Add authentication middleware or token validation before processing network requests.

rules.title: No innerHTML assignment (XSS prevention)
rules.description: CC6.1 (Logical Access): Direct innerHTML assignment enables cross-site scripting, allowing attackers to execute code in user browsers.
rules.fix: Use textContent for plain text, or DOMPurify.sanitize() before setting innerHTML.

rules.title: SQL injection: unsanitized input in queries
rules.description: CC6.1 (Logical Access): User input flowing directly into database queries enables SQL injection attacks.
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries or an ORM with proper input validation.

rules.title: Command injection: unsanitized input in shell
rules.description: CC6.1 (Logical Access): User input flowing into child_process enables OS command injection attacks.
rules.fix: Use child_process.execFile() with explicit arguments array instead of exec().

rules.title: Crypto operations must have error handling
rules.description: CC6.6 (System Operations): Cryptographic operations must be wrapped in error handling to prevent silent failures that could expose sensitive data.
rules.fix: Add try/catch around all cryptographic operations with proper error logging.

rules.title: No console.log in production code
rules.description: A1.2 (Availability): Console statements in production can leak sensitive information and impact performance. Use structured logging with log levels.
rules.fix: Use a structured logging library (Winston, Pino) with proper log levels.

rules.title: Error handlers must re-throw or log
rules.description: A1.2 (Availability): Silent catch blocks hide errors, making availability incidents impossible to detect and diagnose.
rules.fix: Add error logging or re-throw the error in catch blocks.

rules.title: Env access must have validation
rules.description: PI1.3 (Processing Integrity): Direct process.env access without validation can cause undefined behavior. Environment variables must be validated at startup.
rules.fix: Validate environment variables at startup using a config validation library like zod or joi.

rules.title: Functions should not exceed 5 parameters
rules.description: PI1.3 (Processing Integrity): Functions with many parameters are harder to test and more prone to incorrect usage, risking processing integrity.
rules.fix: Use an options/config object pattern instead of many positional parameters.

rules.title: High complexity functions need review
rules.description: PI1.3 (Processing Integrity): Functions with high cyclomatic complexity (>8) are harder to test and more error-prone.
rules.fix: Extract nested logic into helper functions. Consider the single responsibility principle.

rules.title: XSS: unsanitized input in response
rules.description: PI1.3 (Processing Integrity): User input sent directly to HTTP responses enables XSS, corrupting data presented to users.
rules.fix: Sanitize all user input before including it in responses.

rules.title: Path traversal: unsanitized input in file operations
rules.description: C1.1 (Confidentiality): User input flowing into file system operations enables path traversal, potentially exposing confidential data.
rules.fix: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use path.basename() to strip directory traversal.

rules.title: Source files must have copyright header
rules.description: C1.1 (Confidentiality): All source files must include the organization's copyright header to demonstrate intellectual property controls.
rules.fix: Add the standard copyright header to the top of the file.

rules.title: No TODO/FIXME/HACK in production
rules.description: CC7.2 (Change Management): Unresolved TODO comments indicate incomplete implementation that should be tracked as issues before deployment.
rules.fix: Resolve the TODO or convert it to a tracked issue in your project management system.

rules.title: Files must not exceed 500 lines
rules.description: CC7.2 (Change Management): Large files are hard to review in change management processes, increasing risk of undetected issues.
rules.fix: Split into smaller, focused modules. Each file should have a single responsibility.

rules.title: UI layer must not import from data layer
rules.description: CC8.1 (Architecture): UI components should only import from allowed layers. Enforces clean architecture for maintainability and auditability.
rules.fix: Access data through the services layer, not directly from UI components.

rules.title: No circular imports
rules.description: CC8.1 (Architecture): Circular dependencies cause runtime issues and indicate poor module design, impacting system reliability.
rules.fix: Extract shared types/interfaces to a separate module to break the cycle.

rules.title: Async functions must use await
rules.description: CC6.1 (Logical Access): Async functions without await are likely missing intended async operations, potentially bypassing security checks.
rules.fix: Add await calls or remove the async keyword if async is not needed.

rules.title: No multi-line SQL string concatenation
rules.description: P1.1 (Privacy): Multi-line SQL strings built with concatenation are prone to injection, potentially exposing personal data.
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries or an ORM to prevent SQL injection.
